workflow.name: val-validate-artifact

critical.mandate: Val NEVER writes findings to an artifact without explicit user confirmation. The discussion loop (Step 6) is a MANDATORY interaction point.
model_override: opus is architecturally mandated (ADR-012). If the current model is not opus, HALT with a clear error — do NOT silently degrade validation quality.
Findings must be framed diplomatically — Val proposes, the user decides. Use neutral language: "appears to be", "may be outdated", "could not verify" rather than accusatory phrasing.
Each skill section is loaded JIT and released before the next step. Never pre-load skill sections.
For large artifacts, validate section-by-section using the heading map from Step 1. Never load the entire artifact at once for validation.

step.title: Parse Artifact
step.action: Read the target artifact at {artifact_path}
Parse the heading structure (##, ###, ####) into a section map: for each section, record heading level, title, and line range

Determine chunking strategy based on artifact size: — Small artifact (under 200 lines): treat as single chunk, validate all at once — Medium artifact (200–600 lines): chunk by top-level sections (## headings) — Large artifact (over 600 lines): chunk by second-level sections (### headings) for finer granularity
If the artifact has no headings (e.g., flat content): treat the entire artifact as a single chunk
Present the section map to confirm scope: "{N} sections identified, {M} chunks for validation"

step.title: Detect Artifact Type and Run Document-Specific Rules
step.action: Load the "type-detection" section from the document-rulesets skill (JIT)

Extract the basename from {artifact_path} and match against the type-detection mapping table to determine the artifact type and corresponding ruleset ID (prd-rules, arch-rules, ux-rules, test-plan-rules, epics-rules, or unknown)

If artifact type is unknown (no ruleset matches): skip structural rules entirely — no document-specific rules to apply. Log: "No document-specific ruleset for this artifact type — factual verification only." Release the type-detection section and proceed directly to Step 3 (Pass 2 — factual claims only).

If artifact type is recognized: load the matching ruleset section from document-rulesets skill (JIT). Execute Pass 1 — structural rules first: run all checks defined in the ruleset against the artifact content. Record structural findings with source tag [STRUCTURAL]. Release the ruleset section from context.
Release the "type-detection" section from context

step.title: Extract Claims (Pass 2 — Factual Verification)
step.action: Load the "claim-extraction" section from the validation-patterns skill (JIT)

For each chunk from Step 1, extract all verifiable factual claims: — File paths: any reference to a file or directory (e.g., "_gaia/dev/agents/", "docs/planning-artifacts/prd.md") — Component counts: numerical assertions about how many agents, workflows, skills, etc. exist — Agent/workflow/skill references: named references to framework components — FR/ADR cross-references: requirement IDs (FR-*) and architectural decision references (ADR-*) — Version numbers: framework version, module versions, dependency versions — Structural assertions: claims about directory structure, file contents, or configuration values
For each claim, record: claim text, source section, source line (approximate), claim type

If no verifiable factual claims are found in the entire artifact: produce a report stating "No factual claims identified for verification" and skip to workflow completion — do NOT write an empty findings section
Release the "claim-extraction" skill section from context

step.title: Filesystem Verify
step.action: Load the "filesystem-verification" section from the validation-patterns skill (JIT)

For each extracted claim, verify against the actual filesystem: — File path claims: check if the file/directory exists at the stated path using glob or direct read — Count claims: enumerate actual items and compare against the stated count — Content assertions: read the referenced file and verify the stated content matches — Structural claims: verify directory structures match the described layout
For each claim, record verification result: VERIFIED (claim matches reality) or FAILED (claim does not match) with evidence
Release the "filesystem-verification" skill section from context

step.title: Cross-Reference Ground Truth
step.action: Check if {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md exists and is non-empty

If ground-truth.md is missing or empty: skip cross-reference checks. Record a note: "Ground truth not available — cross-reference checks skipped." Proceed to Step 6. Filesystem verification results from Step 4 are still valid.

If ground-truth.md exists and is non-empty: — Load the "cross-reference" section from the validation-patterns skill (JIT) — For each claim that was VERIFIED in Step 4, cross-reference against ground truth: — Check if ground truth contains a contradicting fact — Check if ground truth has a more recent or more precise version of the same fact — Flag any discrepancies between the artifact claim and ground truth — Release the "cross-reference" skill section from context

step.title: Classify Findings
step.action: Load the "severity-classification" section from the validation-patterns skill (JIT)

Compile and merge findings from both passes — structural findings from Step 2 (Pass 1, tagged [STRUCTURAL]) and factual findings from Steps 3-5 (Pass 2, tagged [FACTUAL]). Include only FAILED verifications, ground-truth discrepancies, and structural quality issues.

Classify each finding into exactly one severity level: — **CRITICAL**: Wrong file path (file does not exist), incorrect count (stated N but actual is M), broken reference (FR/ADR/component does not exist), contradicts ground truth on a verified fact — **WARNING**: Outdated reference (file exists but content has changed), stale data (version number is behind), ground truth has newer information — **INFO**: Style suggestion (naming inconsistency), minor inconsistency (non-breaking), cosmetic discrepancy
If no findings were produced (all claims verified, no discrepancies): report "All {N} claims verified — no findings." Skip Steps 7 and 8 — proceed to workflow completion.
Release the "severity-classification" skill section from context

step.title: Discussion Loop
step.action:
Present all classified findings to the user in a structured table:
| # | Severity | Section | Claim | Finding | Evidence | |---|----------|---------|-------|---------|----------| (one row per finding)
Summary: {total} findings — {critical_count} CRITICAL, {warning_count} WARNING, {info_count} INFO
step.template-output: Draft findings table with all classified findings, severity counts, and evidence for each. This is a DRAFT — only user-approved findings will be written to the artifact.
step.action: For each finding, ask the user to confirm or dispute: — APPROVE: finding is correct, include in written output — DISPUTE: finding is incorrect or not applicable, exclude from written output — EDIT: modify the finding description before approving
Only findings explicitly approved by the user proceed to Step 8
If the user disputes ALL findings: report "All findings disputed — no changes written to artifact." Complete the workflow gracefully.

step.title: Write Approved Findings
step.action: Load the "findings-formatting" section from the validation-patterns skill (JIT)
Check if the target artifact already contains a "## Validation Findings" section

If an existing "## Validation Findings" section is found: replace it entirely with the new findings. Do NOT append — each validation run is a fresh assessment. Prior findings are preserved in git history.

Write the approved findings to the target artifact under a new "## Validation Findings" section:
## Validation Findings
> Validated: {date} | Workflow: val-validate-artifact v{framework_version} | Model: opus
| # | Severity | Finding | Reference | |---|----------|---------|-----------| (one row per approved finding — severity tag, one-line summary, what was checked)
Summary: {approved_count} finding(s) from {total_checked} claims verified.
Release the "findings-formatting" skill section from context

step.title: Save to Val Memory
step.action:
Auto-save all validation results to Val's memory sidecar (no user prompt required):
1. Append to {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md using standardized format: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Artifact Validation: {artifact_name}
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** val-validate-artifact - **Sprint:** {sprint_id or "N/A"} - **Status:** recorded - **Related:** {artifact_path}
Validated artifact: {artifact_path}. Claims checked: {total_claims}. Findings: {total_findings} ({critical_count} critical, {warning_count} warning, {info_count} info). Approved: {approved_count}. Disputed: {disputed_count}. {Brief summary of key findings}
2. Replace body of {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md (preserve header above first ---): Last session: Artifact validation of {artifact_name}. Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}. Claims: {total_claims}. Findings: {total_findings}. Approved: {approved_count}. Disputed: {disputed_count}.
If _memory/validator-sidecar/ directory or files don't exist, create them with standard headers: decision-log.md: "# Val Validator — Decision Log

> Chronological record of validation decisions.

---" conversation-context.md: "# Val Validator — Conversation Context

> Rolling summary of the most recent validation session.

---

No sessions recorded yet." If writing fails, log warning and continue — memory save is non-blocking.

workflow.next-step: Suggested next steps: - /gaia-val-validate — run validation again on a different artifact
